texto: Good people are not those who lack flaws, the brave are not those who feel no fear, and the generous are not those who never feel selfish. Extraordinary people are not extraordinary because they are invulnerable to unconscious biases. They are extraordinary because they choose to do something about it.
explicacao: Citação de Shankar Vedantam sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. “Good people are not those who lack flaws, the brave are not those who feel no fear, and the gener…” articula coragem, extraordinary, good em registro de coragem, com relevância para contextos como good e unconscious.

frase_original: Good people are not those who lack flaws, the brave are not those who feel no fear, and the generous are not those who never feel selfish. Extraordinary people are not extraordinary because they are invulnerable to unconscious biases. They are extraordinary because they choose to do something about it.

texto: some students look at the problems that they're facing and they draw global conclusions from them. They say this is not just a professor giving me a bad grade or someone not sitting next to me in the cafeteria. This reflects that fact that I am not ready for college, or I shouldn't be in this college at all.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Shankar Vedantam sobre temas ligados a reflexao. A afirmação “some students look at the problems that they're facing and they draw global conclusions from them…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a reflexao, college, maturity, útil em momentos de maturity e perspective.
